evals.prompt: I want to add SparkScan to my Flutter app. Here's my starter page: EmptyPage.dart. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete SparkScan integration code into EmptyPage.dart (BLoC + page widget), and shows the setup checklist (pub packages to add, flutter pub get, iOS Info.plist, Android runtime permission).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions scandit_flutter_datacapture_barcode and flutter pub get)
Setup checklist mentions iOS NSCameraUsageDescription in ios/Runner/Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions Android runtime camera permission (via permission_handler)
await ScanditFlutterDataCaptureBarcode.initialize() is present in main() before runApp
WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized() is called before the await
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( OR DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
License key placeholder (e.g. YOUR_LICENSE_KEY or similar) is present
SparkScan(settings: ...) is used (v8 constructor form, not SparkScan.withSettings or SparkScan.forSettings)
Symbology.ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.code128 is enabled
No symbologies except ean13Upca and code128 are enabled
SparkScanView.forContext( is used to create the view
sparkScan.addListener( is called to register the scan listener
A class implements SparkScanListener with didScan and didUpdateSession overrides
Imports come from scandit_flutter_datacapture_core (DataCaptureContext) and scandit_flutter_datacapture_barcode (SparkScan, SparkScanView, SparkScanSettings, Symbology)

evals.prompt: Add SparkScan barcode scanning to ProductListBloc.dart. When a barcode is scanned, add it to the scannedItems list and emit through the existing stream.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds SparkScan to ProductListBloc.dart alongside the existing stream/state code. All existing stream controller, list, and clear logic are preserved.

assertions.text: The BLoC implements SparkScanListener
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( OR DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
SparkScan(settings: ...) is used
SparkScanView.forContext( or reference to it is present (the BLoC exposes sparkScan/context for the page to build the view)
sparkScan.addListener(this) is called
didScan override is present
didScan appends to the existing scannedItems list
Existing scannedItems list is preserved
Existing stream controller (_scannedItemsController) or equivalent is preserved and emits on new scans
Existing clear() method is preserved

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my Flutter app, I want to reject it if the data is '123456789' and show an error message 'Wrong barcode' for 30 seconds before resuming scanning. All other barcodes should show success feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill makes the BLoC implement SparkScanFeedbackDelegate, overrides feedbackForBarcode to return SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback for rejected barcodes and SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback for valid ones, and assigns feedbackDelegate on the sparkScanView in the page.

assertions.text: The BLoC implements SparkScanFeedbackDelegate
feedbackForBarcode override is present
SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback( or SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback.fromMessage( is returned for rejected barcodes
Duration(seconds: 30) (or equivalent) is passed as the resumeCapturingDelay
SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback() is returned for valid barcodes
The rejection condition checks barcode.data == '123456789' or equivalent
sparkScanView.feedbackDelegate is assigned (either in the page build or on creation)
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to hide the standard SparkScan trigger button and instead use a custom Flutter FloatingActionButton in my app's UI. When the custom button is tapped, scanning should start. When tapped again while scanning, it should pause.
evals.expected_output: The skill hides the standard trigger button (triggerButtonVisible = false), wires up a custom FAB, and its onPressed handler calls sparkScanView.startScanning() and sparkScanView.pauseScanning().

assertions.text: triggerButtonVisible = false is set on the SparkScanView (either via constructor named param or property assignment)
A FloatingActionButton (or equivalent Flutter button) is added to the page
onPressed handler is wired to the FAB
sparkScanView.startScanning() is called inside the handler
sparkScanView.pauseScanning() is called for the pause branch
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to change the SparkScan UI look for my Flutter app. Hide the camera switch, hide the zoom switch, and set the trigger button tint color to white.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets the appropriate visibility and color properties on the SparkScanView instance using v8 property names.

assertions.text: cameraSwitchButtonVisible = false is set
zoomSwitchControlVisible = false is set
triggerButtonTintColor is assigned a Color value (Colors.white or Color(0xFFFFFFFF))
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener) is preserved
Old v6 property names (captureButtonTintColor, torchButtonVisible) are NOT present

evals.prompt: I need custom behavior when the user taps the barcode count button in the SparkScan toolbar — I want to navigate to a summary screen. Wire this up.
evals.expected_output: The skill installs a SparkScanViewUiListener on the sparkScanView and implements didTapBarcodeCountButton to trigger the navigation.

assertions.text: A class implements SparkScanViewUiListener (may be the State class itself)
didTapBarcodeCountButton is overridden
Navigator.of(context).push / pushNamed (or equivalent Flutter navigation) is invoked inside the handler
The listener is assigned to the view (sparkScanView.setListener(...) or .uiListener)
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to clean up properly when my Flutter page is disposed — remove the SparkScan listener and close the scan stream so resources are released.
evals.expected_output: The skill wires sparkScan.removeListener and StreamController.close() into the BLoC/state dispose() chain.

assertions.text: A dispose() method exists on the BLoC / controller
sparkScan.removeListener(this) is called inside dispose()
The StreamController (scan events) is closed inside dispose()
The page State.dispose() invokes the BLoC's dispose()
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to filter scans so duplicates within 500 milliseconds are ignored. Update my SparkScan settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets codeDuplicateFilter on the SparkScanSettings instance (as a Duration) before constructing the SparkScan mode.

assertions.text: settings.codeDuplicateFilter is set to Duration(milliseconds: 500) (or equivalent in the API units)
codeDuplicateFilter is set BEFORE SparkScan(settings: settings) is constructed
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I'm building a high-volume warehouse scanning app where we need rapid, continuous barcode scanning without pausing between items. My app already has SparkScan integrated: IntegratedBloc.dart. Reconfigure it with CONTINUOUS scanning behavior and PERSISTENT preview so the camera stays visible the whole time.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures SparkScanViewSettings with defaultScanningMode set to SparkScanScanningModeDefault.fromPreviewBehavior(SparkScanScanningBehavior.continuous, SparkScanPreviewBehavior.persistent), keeping the scanner active and camera always visible, and passes the settings to SparkScanView.

assertions.text: SparkScanViewSettings is created or configured
defaultScanningMode is set on SparkScanViewSettings
SparkScanScanningModeDefault.fromPreviewBehavior( is used
SparkScanScanningBehavior.continuous is used (not single)
SparkScanPreviewBehavior.persistent is used (not defaultBehaviour)
The configured SparkScanViewSettings is passed to the SparkScanView widget
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan mode, listener, BLoC structure) is preserved
